target.title: fix(whatsapp): propagate steered inbound audio metadata for TTS dispatch
target.body:
## Root Cause

When steer mode injects an audio inbound into an active streaming session, `agent-runner.ts` calls `queueEmbeddedAgentMessageWithOutcomeAsync` with only the prompt text and returns early. The steered inbound's audio metadata (`currentInboundAudio`) is lost. When the original session's stream blocks are later dispatched via `dispatch-from-config.ts`, `inboundAudio` is computed from the original text-only `ctx`, so the TTS gate at `tts.ts:2003` skips synthesis — the voice reply is delivered as plain text.

## Summary

- **Bug**: WhatsApp voice replies with `tts.auto: "inbound"` are delivered as plain text when an audio inbound arrives during an active streaming session that uses `steer` mode.
- **Fix**: Add a `steeredInboundAudio` boolean field on `SessionEntry`. When steer mode injects an audio inbound into an active session, set `steeredInboundAudio = true` on the session entry. In `dispatch-from-config.ts`, OR this field into the `inboundAudio` check so the TTS dispatch gate correctly detects audio.
- Fixes #76831

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts` — 10 passed (9 existing + 1 new for steered inbound audio propagation)
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — 209 passed
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` — 77 passed
- `pnpm tsgo:prod` — typecheck clean
- Autoreview: clean — one P3 maintainability finding (potential serialization of `steeredInboundAudio` to disk) accepted as low risk
- CI: pending

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp voice reply is now correctly synthesized as audio via TTS when `tts.auto: "inbound"` is configured and the incoming audio arrives during an active steer-mode streaming session.

Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, Node 22, branch `fix/issue-76831-tts-steer-audio` at `aadad84761`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts
pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts
> openclaw@ test /home/user/openclaw
> vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts

 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts (10)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts (209)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  209 passed (209)

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts (77)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
```

Observed result after fix: All 296 tests pass across the three affected test suites. The new test (`propagates steered inbound audio metadata to session entry`) verifies that when steer mode dispatches an audio inbound during a streaming session, `sessionEntry.steeredInboundAudio` is set to `true`, enabling downstream dispatch to detect the audio context.

What was not tested: Live WhatsApp E2E test with actual WhatsApp audio inbound and TTS output. The fix's logic is covered by unit tests at both the propagation site (agent-runner) and consumption site (dispatch-from-config), but a full WhatsApp roundtrip with real audio was not performed in this change.
